text: Developing a mobile app involves several important decisions. First, developers must choose between native or cross-platform development. Native apps are built specifically for iOS or Android, offering better performance and deeper device integration. Cross-platform tools like React Native allow one codebase to work on both systems, saving time but sometimes sacrificing responsiveness. Responsive design ensures the app adapts smoothly to different screen sizes, from phones to tablets. Once ready, the app is submitted to app stores like the Apple App Store or Google Play, which have strict guidelines. Performance is crucial—users expect fast loading, smooth animations, and minimal battery drain. Testing on real devices helps achieve this.
